title: nepeta pseudokoreana
intro: Nepeta pseudokoreana is a plant species of the genus Nepeta, commonly known as catmint or beebalm. It is a perennial herb native to mountainous regions of East Asia, known for its distinctive purple flowers and medicinal properties. Nepeta pseudokoreana contains compounds with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including relief from respiratory issues, digestive problems, and skin irritations. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, to leverage its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
